# Rex

> PRD management and implementation workflow CLI for AI-assisted development.

Rex tracks product requirements as a tree of epics, features, tasks, and subtasks. It provides both a CLI for humans and an MCP server for AI agents.

## Usage

```
rex init [dir]              Initialize .rex/ directory
rex status [dir]            Print PRD tree with stats
rex next [dir]              Get next actionable task
rex add <level> [dir]       Add item (epic|feature|task|subtask)
rex update <id> [dir]       Update item status/priority
rex validate [dir]          Check PRD integrity
rex recommend [dir]         SourceVision recommendations
rex analyze [dir]           Build PRD from project analysis
rex mcp [dir]               Start MCP server (stdio)
```

## Data model

Items form a strict hierarchy: epic > feature > task > subtask. Each item has an id (UUID), title, status (pending|in_progress|completed|deferred), level, and optional description, acceptanceCriteria, priority (critical|high|medium|low), tags, source, blockedBy (dependency IDs), and children.

The PRD document is stored as `.rex/prd.json`:
```json
{ "schema": "rex/v1", "title": "...", "items": [...] }
```

## MCP tools

- get_prd_status: returns title, overall stats, per-epic stats
- get_next_task: returns next actionable item and parent chain
- update_task_status(id, status): change item status
- add_item(title, level, parentId?, description?, priority?, acceptanceCriteria?, tags?, source?, blockedBy?): create item
- get_item(id): item details with parent chain
- append_log(event, itemId?, detail?): write to execution log
- get_capabilities: schema version, adapter, feature flags

## MCP resources

- rex://prd: full PRDDocument as JSON
- rex://workflow: workflow instructions as markdown
- rex://log: last 50 log entries as JSON

## Analyze command

`rex analyze` scans test files (describe/it blocks), docs (markdown headings, JSON title fields, YAML), and SourceVision data (zones, inventory, imports) to propose PRD items. Use --lite for filename-only scanning, --accept to add proposals to the PRD. Reconciles against existing items to skip duplicates.

## Workflow

Rex's default agent workflow: get next task, implement with TDD, mark complete, log decisions, commit, exit. One task per execution.

## File structure

```
.rex/
  config.json           # { schema, project, adapter, sourcevision }
  prd.json              # PRD tree
  execution-log.jsonl   # append-only event log
  workflow.md           # agent workflow instructions
```
